Harvester  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
AC: 4
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 CHA: -5 INT: +5
Effect: Deadeye (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 8.0 Size: LARGE
Class: NEC
Race: HUM ERU DEF GNM IKS FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
AC: 4
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

Effect: Deadeye (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 8.0 Size: LARGE
Class: NEC
Race: HUM ERU DEF GNM IKS FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Lore:Harvester of Shadows
Item Type:Misc
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:13 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:824
Submitted By:Gelf Grifclaw

Source:Live

Page Updated:Tue Apr 11th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 600pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 30
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
The Harvester

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Temple of Solusek Ro
Has the appearance of a scythe.


Uploaded November 27th, 2008

▲ Page top
it's all about the look...
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2002 at 1:57 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759911393
61 posts
sure these stats are only mediocre (although well picked and balanced for the intended class), and my necro is a d elf that has little use for the effect, (but then again, you can never over look the potential use of a mana free see invis). but hell, i'd like to have this to just equip while running around socializing, necros look bada** when carring a big ol' scythe! hehe 8P

all and all, definately a sweet item if your not twinked and well worth picking up, even if it is just for how much more awesome you will look while carring it, and to save you from memming and casting deadeye every time you are not sure if you aren't seeing somethin.
▲ Page top
RE: it's all about the look...
QuoteReply
# Jan 09 2003 at 3:27 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591782793772
Scholar
435 posts
I've skipped this quest for quite a while, despite having the original note, but at level 41 the prospect of a free primary item with +5INT is looking pretty good; I now rarely get anywhere near the mobs I'm fighting so I don't need a weapon, and although it weighs a lot, with the +5 STR it's effectively no heavier than my current dagger or smoldering brand.

Edit: for that matter, I'm still carrying it at 57 :). There are plenty of other items I could get, but the permanent see-invis is so handy that I don't want to trade this one in yet. Unfortunately the Bobbing Corpse spell on my right-click ear-ring doesn't seem to include see invis, unlike DMF... plus there are just too many zones where those spells can't be cast.

Edited, Fri Nov 14 07:33:52 2003
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Jun 15 2002 at 11:20 AM Rating: Decent

QuoteReply
# Feb 11 2002 at 10:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756200153
6 posts
handed in the syth at the same time I handed in the book for the words quest, now I have 2 notes which are identicle, even identify the same has anyone else had this problem and how did they get around it? (I bet the notes aren't interchangeable).
▲ Page top
RE: Help
QuoteReply
# May 08 2002 at 6:30 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765394668
1 post
Just read text after you hand in the items. It says, just hand him the note, and he will give you the item.

Ozadar of the 35th Corpse
▲ Page top
Easy quest (heh..for a 51)
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2002 at 11:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767089005
View User Forum
212 posts
I just finished this quest for my guild last night.
As a 51 druid, I can say it was easy. At mid 20's, not sure.
Estate of unrest sucks, as they all see undead, and the werebats see through ITU...
I was (stupidly) fighting each damn mob that aggroed me, until I casted wolf, and nothing attacked, making that so much easier.
Paid 72pp for fire opal...

▲ Page top
Burrfoot
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2001 at 9:36 AM Rating: Excellent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Yep. :)

Turn the shadowed scythe in first: this gets you the note. Then turn in the note, shadowed eye, shadow dagger and fire opal all at the same time.

Shadow Dagger - The goblin headmaster (another guide says headhunter, but it's headmaster) spawns on the island across from the Sisters on Ocean of Tears. Around on the back side of the island there's a hut standing near a large stone pillar/totem. The headmaster pops there, just clear out the area and wait. :)

Fire Opal - Bought in the Temple, she says she'll take gold only, but she will take platinum as well. (55pp)

Shadowed Scythe/Shadowed Eye - Easiest thing to do is just kill the shadowed man camp in Lavastorm. 6 Shadowed men spawn there, so it's a decent spawn rate. Smack em till what you need drops, then it's just a hop skip and jump to the Temple.

G'luck!
▲ Page top
iksars can use! (NT)
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2001 at 8:37 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
▲ Page top
Make sure you...
QuoteReply
# Mar 21 2001 at 1:40 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I did this quest last night. Just make sure you don't just hand in everything at once. I made the mistake (which was really stupid cause every other time I've hailed the person when I've done a temple quest) cause you hand in the shadowed scythe first & get a note. Hand in the note & eye, fire opal & shadow daggar & you get the harvester.

Simple mistake but could ruin your day :-)
▲ Page top
RE: Make sure you...
QuoteReply
